boundary.summary: Well-researched audit and policy design, but scope-creeps three deliverables into one plan

strengths: Audit correctly identifies 8 existing narrow-scoped mechanisms with verified code evidence
5-layer architecture is coherent and addresses a genuine cross-cutting security gap
Companion findings artifact (rl-004-findings.v1.json) provides file:line evidence for each gap
weaknesses: Plan simultaneously tries to be: (1) retrospective audit, (2) forward policy, (3) implementation roadmap — three different deliverables
No single implementation wave could execute even one full layer without concrete file paths, interface definitions, and sequencing
Claims 'all packages covered' but doesn't prioritize which package's gaps should be addressed first
No explicit scope exclusion — nothing is called out as 'out of scope for this plan'
code_evidence: sanitizedProcessEnv (packages/core/src/util/opencode-process.ts:19-24) confirmed: passes ALL env vars unfiltered — claim A accurate
createSidecarEnv (packages/desktop/src/main/server.ts:230-237) confirmed: only strips DEBUG and LD_PRELOAD — claim A accurate
sanitizeSession (packages/opencode/src/session/session-export.ts:32-50) confirmed: extracts metadata only, no value scanning
redactConfig (packages/opencode/src/debug/debug-packet.ts:124-143) confirmed: key-name matching only, no value scanning

surface_area.summary: Covers all 13+ packages — far too broad for a single implementation plan

strengths: Comprehensive audit surface gives a complete risk picture
weaknesses: Implementation would touch at minimum 15+ files across 5+ packages
No proposed file-level scope for first implementation slice
Layer 3 (Scrubbing) alone would touch tool output, debug packets, session exports, and log files — each a different subsystem

estimated_impact.layer_2_prevention: 3+ spawn sites across packages/core and packages/desktop
estimated_impact.layer_3_scrubbing: 4+ output paths across packages/opencode and packages/desktop
estimated_impact.layer_5_integrity: Credential stores across packages/opencode and packages/desktop

reversibility.summary: 5-layer design is inherently good for independence, but no dependency ordering is specified

strengths: Each of the 5 layers can theoretically be implemented and rolled back independently
Layer 2 (Prevention/ENV_BLOCKLIST) is mechanically independent — it's a filter applied at subprocess spawn
Layer 4 (Guarding/file I/O protection) is entirely additive — new checks that can be toggled off
weaknesses: No explicit dependency ordering between layers (Layer 1 is prerequisite for Layer 3, Layer 2 is prerequisite for Layer 5)
No file-level grouping defined — changes within a layer could span 5+ files in different packages, making partial revert risky
Package ownership undecided — if implemented in wrong package, revert requires cross-package coordination

coupling.summary: Centralization direction is correct, but ownership of shared utilities is unaddressed

strengths: Current state has 8 ad-hoc, non-interoperable mechanisms — centralization is the right direction
Replacing 8 bespoke mechanisms with one pipeline reduces coupling across the system
weaknesses: No decision on which package owns the shared detection/prevention utilities
If placed in packages/opencode, packages/enterprise and packages/slack would gain a security dependency on the core engine — unwanted coupling
If placed in packages/core, the coupling is clean (all packages already depend on core), but the plan doesn't say this
No module-level API design — what does 'import { ENV_BLOCKLIST, sanitizeProcessEnv } from "@opencode-ai/core/secrets"' look like?

convergence.summary: Directionally aligned with PG migration credential isolation, but doesn't reference or compose with existing work

strengths: 5-layer unified design moves the system away from ad-hoc per-site patterns — correct direction
Database security doctrine already defines credential isolation pattern (Redacted types, Effect Config, avoiding process.env spreads) — RL-004 extends this to all secrets, not just DB creds
Database credential isolation evidence (docs/json/opencode/evidence/database_credential_isolation.v1.json) in candidate_local status — existing work RL-004 complements
weaknesses: Plan does not reference database_security_doctrine.v1.json or credential isolation evidence — risks parallel incompatible solutions
database_security_doctrine Phase 2.0 already plans a 'credential isolation audit' of subprocess spawns — RL-004 proposes the same audit from scratch

Redacted type pattern (database_security_doctrine) and ENV_BLOCKLIST pattern (RL-004 Layer 2) need to compose: secrets stored as Redacted should also be in the blocklist. Plan doesn't address this composition
No cross-reference to existing migration plans (pg_backend_migration, final_closures) to ensure secrets policy timing aligns with architecture rollout

verified_accurate: A. sanitizedProcessEnv passes ALL env vars — CONFIRMED (packages/core/src/util/opencode-process.ts:19)
B. No binary/file-read security — CONFIRMED (no isLikelyBinary/resolveSafePath in packages)
C. No bash security rerouting — CONFIRMED (no reroute pattern in packages)
D. No general-purpose scrubber — CONFIRMED (redactConfig is key-name only)
E. No central ENV_BLOCKLIST — CONFIRMED (no such constant exists)
1. HTTP Recorder redaction — CONFIRMED (packages/http-recorder/src/redaction.ts, HTTP recording only)
2. LLM Provider error redaction — not verified (need packages/llm access)
3. Debug packet config redaction — CONFIRMED (packages/opencode/src/debug/debug-packet.ts:124, key-name only)
7. Session export sanitization — PARTIAL: sanitizeSession extracts metadata only, no message content scanning — plan's description is generous
8. Proxy header sanitization — not verified (need proxy-util.ts access)

recommendations: Decompose into 3-5 narrow implementation plans, each scoped to one layer or one critical gap
First slice: Layer 2 (ENV_BLOCKLIST + sanitizeProcessEnv rename) — touches 3 files, clear scope, immediate impact
Specify package ownership: recommend packages/core for shared utilities, not packages/opencode
Add dependency ordering between layers before any implementation starts
Cross-reference database_security_doctrine.v1.json and compose RL-004 Layer 2 with existing Redacted+Effect Config credential isolation pattern
Provide concrete type/interface definitions for the shared utilities before implementation begins
follow_up_issues: Create narrow plan for Layer 2: ENV_BLOCKLIST in packages/core + filter in cross-spawn-spawner + server.ts + ripgrep.ts
Create narrow plan for Layer 4: isLikelyBinary + resolveSafePath for file-read tools
Create architecture decision record for secrets utility package ownership
Audit process.env reads across all packages to catalog every secret consumption point (complements RL-004 audit)
